advisory.title: ISA-2025-001: Non-deterministic JSON Unmarshalling of IBC Acknowledgement can result in a chain halt
advisory.description: ibc-go advisory ISA-2025-001 extends the protections introduced by ASA-2025-004 (which was scoped to the transfer module) to all IBC applications. A non-deterministic JSON unmarshalling bug in IBC acknowledgement processing in modules/core/04-channel/keeper/packet.go can produce divergent acknowledgement bytes across nodes and halt the chain. Any party that can open an IBC channel can introduce the failing state. Fixed in v7.10.0 and v8.7.0.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v8.6.1 release commit, the last vulnerable release before v8.7.0 (the ISA-2025-001 patched version). v8.6.1 already contains the ASA-2025-004 transfer-module fix but still has the non-deterministic decoding path in modules/core/04-channel/keeper/packet.go. The introducing commit is the original commit that established the modules/ directory tree where packet.go lives; the v8.6.1..v8.7.0 patch modifies packet.go to extend deterministic acknowledgement handling to all applications.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag non-deterministic JSON unmarshalling of IBC acknowledgement bytes in modules/core/04-channel/keeper/packet.go that can produce divergent state across nodes and halt the chain.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: ISA-2025-001 (GHSA-4wf3-5qj9-368v) was published on 2025-03-12 with patches in ibc-go v7.10.0 and v8.7.0; it extends the earlier ASA-2025-004 transfer-only fix to all IBC applications.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v8.6.1 release commit, the latest tag in the <8.7.0 vulnerable range for v8.x.
